clauses.text: This Agreement is entered into between [CLIENT_NAME] ("Client") and [PROVIDER_NAME] ("Service Provider").
This Agreement governs the provision of [PROJECT_NAME] by the Service Provider to the Client.

The Service Provider shall perform search engine optimization services described in [SERVICES_CONTRACTED] for [WEBSITE_URL]. The Service Provider does not guarantee any specific search engine ranking position, as rankings are determined by search engines using criteria outside either Party's control.
The Client shall pay the Service Provider [TOTAL_VALUE] for the Services, payable via [PAYMENT_METHOD] according to the schedule agreed between the Parties.
If the Client fails to pay any amount due by its due date, the Service Provider may charge interest on the overdue amount at [LATE_PAYMENT_RATE] per month.

The Service Provider shall deliver a baseline audit of [WEBSITE_URL] within [BASELINE_AUDIT_PERIOD_DAYS] days of this Agreement's start, and thereafter a report per [BILLING_PERIOD] summarizing work performed and changes in [SEO_METRICS_TRACKED] since the baseline.

Where the Services include content production for SEO purposes, the Service Provider shall provide [CONTENT_PIECES_PER_PERIOD] pieces per [BILLING_PERIOD] for an additional fee of [CONTENT_PRODUCTION_FEE].

Where the Services include technical on-site implementation, the Client shall provide the Service Provider with the access described in [WEBSITE_ACCESS_REQUIREMENTS] within [ACCESS_PROVISION_PERIOD_DAYS] days of request. The Service Provider shall not be liable for delay resulting from the Client's failure to provide this access.
The Parties shall meet each [BILLING_PERIOD] to review the Reports Clause deliverables and plan upcoming activity.
Audits requested beyond the baseline audit described in the Reports Clause shall be charged at [ADDITIONAL_AUDIT_FEE] per audit.
Where the Services include training, the Service Provider shall provide [TRAINING_HOURS] hours of training on SEO practices relevant to [WEBSITE_URL].
The Service Provider shall deliver the Deliverables to the Client on or before [DELIVERY_DATE].
The Services shall take place on [DELIVERY_DATE] at , subject to any change confirmed in writing by both Parties.

Each Party shall keep confidential any non-public information disclosed by the other Party in connection with this Agreement and shall not disclose it without the disclosing Party's prior written consent.
Neither Party shall be liable to the other for any indirect, incidental, or consequential loss arising from this Agreement.
Each Party's total liability arising from this Agreement shall not exceed [TOTAL_VALUE], except for loss arising from gross negligence, willful misconduct, or fraud.

Neither Party shall be liable for a failure to perform its obligations to the extent that failure results from an event beyond that Party's reasonable control that could not have been avoided through reasonable diligence. The affected Party shall notify the other Party without undue delay.
This Agreement shall be governed by [APPLICABLE_LAW]. Any dispute shall be submitted to [DISPUTE_RESOLUTION_MECHANISM] before litigation, except where interim injunctive relief is required.

The Parties have executed this Agreement as of [SIGNATURE_DATE]. Ambiguity: none — SEO Scope Clause disclaims ranking-position guarantees within the primary scope definition, explaining the rationale (third-party algorithm control) directly in the operative text, addressing Step 2 risk #1 more explicitly than the generic no-guarantee language used in Digital Marketing/Paid Advertising given SEO's heightened deceptive-practice sensitivity. Duplication: none — Reports (baseline + ongoing measurement) and Additional Audits (extra one-off audits) address distinct concerns. Loophole closed: Technical Implementation explicitly shifts delay liability to the Client when access isn't provided in time, closing Step 2 risk #3. Contradiction: none. Missing protection: baseline audit requirement added, ensuring the Reports Clause has a fixed comparison point rather than only forward-looking reports. Complexity: kept Content Production distinct from core SEO Scope, consistent with the Market's pattern of separating content creation from optimization/management work. Enforceability: access provision period and baseline audit period both explicit Dynamic Fields. Attack: Client claims the Provider promised a #1 ranking and sues after failing to achieve it — mitigated by the explicit, rationale-backed disclaimer in the SEO Scope Clause. Attack: Client never grants CMS or server access, then blames the Provider for lack of technical progress — mitigated by Technical Implementation's explicit liability shift for access delay. Attack: Provider claims improvement without any baseline to compare against — mitigated by the mandatory baseline audit in the Reports Clause. No further gaps found. Version 1.0.0 — Self-Review and Red Team passed — Pending Human Legal Review.
